results.title: Centralize MCP Server Management with mtarsier | Neha . posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
Getting “token limit exceeded” in 𝐂𝐥𝐚𝐮𝐝𝐞… even when your prompt is tiny?
Super frustrating — I’ve faced this myself. You barely typed anything… and boom, tokens exhausted in seconds. 🤦 Am sure every claude enthusiast had faced the same.
Here’s what’s actually happening if you carefully read and scan the logs 👇
It’s not just your prompt
It’s also everything attached to it
Every MCP server quietly adds:
• Tool schemas
• Function definitions
• Hidden context (sent every single turn)
𝐒𝐨 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 “𝐬𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐭” 𝐢𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐬𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥
Add a few MCP servers “just in case” →
and suddenly you hit limits way faster than expected
👉 That irritation? It’s real — and avoidable
👉 Hidden context (MCP) is often the culprit
Most users try to shorten prompts 🙇♂️
Smart users reduce unnecessary MCP tools 🫡 [...] Day 1 of HLD: Consistent Hashing
Ravi just got his first big break.
His app suddenly had thousands of users 🎉
He had 3 servers. Life was easy.
“Just do userID % 3,” he said confidently.
And it worked.
⸻
A week later… growth hit hard 🚀
Users doubled. Ravi smiled and added one more server.
Next morning — panic 😨
• Users were logged out
• Cache was useless
• System slowed down
Ravi didn’t understand…
“I just added ONE server. Why is everything broken?”
⸻
That night, his friend Meena explained:
“Ravi, your system is like assigning people to rooms using numbers.
If you change the number of rooms, everyone has to move.”
Ravi paused.
Meena continued:
“Instead, imagine a circular road 🛣️
• Place your servers at different points on the road
• Place users on the same road [...] View profile for Dimas Brizuela

How I Built mcpman — A Universal MCP Server Package Manager for 10+ AI IDEs
Created by Trần Hoàng Tú
When I first started working with the Model Context Protocol (MCP), I noticed something frustrating: each AI IDE had its own way of managing MCP servers. Claude Desktop had one config format, VS Code needed another, Cursor required yet another approach.
That's when I built mcpman — a universal pa...

The missing link

## Your AI agent could be doingso much more.

The right MCP servers and skills unlock research, memory, coding, browsing, and automation. But finding and wiring them up is hard and chaotic.mTarsier fixes the whole chain.

✕ Without mTarsier✓ With mTarsier

✕Searching docs and Reddit to find a useful MCP server or skill

✓Browse a curated marketplace — install in one click

✕Hand-editing JSON scattered across ~/Library, ~/.cursor, ~/.codeium…

✓One unified editor for every client, with live validation

✕Finding out your config broke hours later — no error, no warning

✓Auto-backup before every change. Roll back in one click. [...] ✓Auto-backup before every change. Roll back in one click.

✕Sharing your MCP and skills setup with teammates means sending JSON files and hoping they get it right

✓Export a .tsr snapshot — teammates import it in one click, every client pre-configured

Unlock your agent's potential

Free forever · No account required · macOS, Windows, Linux

results.title: About | mTarsier
results.content:
## Open Source

mTarsier is fully open-source and licensed under the MIT License. The entire codebase is available onGitHub, where you can review the code, report issues, or contribute improvements.

We believe in transparency and community-driven development. Your contributions, feedback, and ideas help make mTarsier better for everyone.

## Technology

mTarsier is built with modern technologies:

 Rust: High-performance backend built with Tauri
 React: Modern, responsive user interface
 TypeScript: Type-safe development
 Tailwind CSS: Beautiful, customizable styling

## Privacy & Security [...] ← Back to Home

mTarsier Logo

# About mTarsier

## What is mTarsier?

mTarsier is a free, open-source desktop application designed to simplify the management of MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers across multiple AI clients. Whether you're using Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, or other AI tools, mTarsier provides a unified interface to configure, manage, and monitor all your MCP connections in one place.

## Why mTarsier?

Managing MCP servers across different AI clients used to be chaotic. Each client has its own configuration file, stored in different locations, with different formats. Making changes meant editing JSON files manually, risking syntax errors, and keeping track of which servers are installed where. [...] ## Privacy & Security

mTarsier runs entirely on your local machine. We don't collect personal data, track your usage, or access your MCP configurations. All your data stays on your device. Read ourPrivacy Policy for more details.

## Conservation

The tarsier is one of the world's smallest and most endangered primates. By naming our project after this remarkable creature, we hope to raise awareness about conservation efforts.Learn more about tarsiers.

## Community

Join our growing community of developers and AI enthusiasts:

 GitHub - Star the repo, report issues, or contribute code
 Follow us on X -@0fficialRohit · @rege\_dev

## Maintainers

mTarsier is maintained by@0fficialRohit and @rege\_dev, with contributions from the open-source community.
